title: Cyclopeltis presliana
intro: Cyclopeltis presliana, also known as the "Cyclopeltis vine," is a type of tropical plant native to Southeast Asia, classified under the family Lomariopsidaceae and belonging to the order Polypodiales. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Cyclopeltis presliana contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
taxonomy: Cyclopeltis presliana is classified in the family Arumaceae, commonly known as the aroid family, which comprises around 3,000 species of flowering plants. The genus Cyclopeltis is a small group of tropical vines. The classification of Cyclopeltis presliana places it within the monocots, a group of flowering plants that have a single cotyledon in their seeds. Further classification within the monocots includes the subclass Alismatidae, a group of aquatic and terrestrial plants with similar characteristics. The species is also often referred to as a member of the order Alismatales, which comprises similar plant species.
